title: Add Coverage Type Information to Annotation Output
body:
### Summary
When reviewing the annotation output generated by `verilator_coverage` with the `--annotate-points` option, it is sometimes possible to infer whether the reported coverage is `line`, `branch`, `expression`, or `toggle` coverage.  
However, depending on the content of the comment field, the coverage type is not always immediately clear.

For example, consider the following toggle coverage entry:  
```
+000003  point: comment=tmp[1]:0->1 hier=coverage_dut_tb.dut  
```
If a `type` field were added before the `comment`, like this:  
```
+000003  point: type=toggle comment=tmp[1]:0->1 hier=coverage_dut_tb.dut  
```
It would make the coverage type much clearer and easier to understand.  

Having the coverage type explicitly included in the annotation output would greatly improve readability and usability of the report.

### Current Behavior
```
+000013  point: comment=clk:0->1 hier=coverage_dut_tb.dut  // Toggle coverage ?  
+000011  point: comment=(rst_n==1) => 0 hier=coverage_dut_tb.dut // Expression coverage ?  
+000004  point: comment=if hier=coverage_dut_tb.dut // Branch coverage ?
```

### Expected Behavior
```
+000013  point: type=toggle comment=clk:0->1 hier=coverage_dut_tb.dut  
+000011  point: type=expr comment=(rst_n==1) => 0 hier=coverage_dut_tb.dut  
+000004  point: type=branch comment=if hier=coverage_dut_tb.dut
```

### Issue
[Add Coverage Type Information to Annotation Output #7131](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/7131)
